channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Greg Lynn refused bail ahead of retrial for campground murder charges
item.description: Greg Lynn, the man accused of committing murders at a campground, has been refused bail as he awaits his upcoming retrial. The decision was made by the court on March 5, 2026, following concerns about...

item.title: Family of Barnsley woman who died from rabies describe traumatic deterioration at inquest
item.description: The family of Yvonne Ford, a 59-year-old grandmother from Barnsley, South Yorkshire, have spoken at an inquest in Sheffield about the devastating impact of her death from rabies. Mrs Ford was scratche...

item.title: Sahel Countries Strengthen Military Surveillance Through Satellites
item.description: Di Sahel countries for West Africa don dey push ground when e come to satellite surveillance, especially afta dem relations with French and American militaries scatter. Dem don start to form new partn...

item.title: Pentagon Identifies Six U.S. Soldiers Killed in Iranian Attack in Kuwait
item.description: The Pentagon has identified six U.S. service members killed in a surprise Iranian attack on a tactical operation center at Kuwait’s Shuaiba port. The soldiers, all from the 103rd Sustainment Command o...

item.title: Kenya Red Cross Volunteer Samuel Wanyonyi Dies While Saving Lives at Mbagathi River
item.description: The Kenya Red Cross Society is in deep mourning following the tragic loss of their veteran aqua rescue volunteer, Samuel Wanyonyi Wangila. Samuel had served with the organisation for over 15 years, ga...

item.title: Philippine inflation rises to 2.4% in February, showing faster price growth
item.description: The Philippine Statistics Authority (PSA) reported that inflation in the Philippines increased to 2.4 percent in February 2026, up from the previous month. This marks a faster growth in consumer price...

item.title: Nigerien Military Drone Strike in Kokoloko Market Kills 17 Civilians and 3 Fighters, HRW Calls for Investigation
item.description: Human Rights Watch don report say on January 6, 2026, one Nigerien military drone strike for Kokoloko village inside Tillabéri region kill at least 17 civilians, including 4 small children, and injure...

item.title: Jamaat-i-Islami Calls for Nationwide Protests in Solidarity with Iran After US-Israel Strikes
item.description: Jamaat-i-Islami Pakistan Emir Hafiz Naeemur Rehman has declared nationwide protests scheduled for Friday to strongly condemn the US and Israel attacks on Iran and to express full solidarity with the I...

item.title: Breakdown of allocations in Kenya's Sh245.9 billion supplementary budget
item.description: The Kenyan government has approved a Sh245.9 billion supplementary budget to address urgent needs across various sectors. This mini-budget allocates funds for critical areas like education, health, in...

item.title: House Oversight Committee Calls Pam Bondi to Testify on Epstein Documents
item.description: The U.S. House of Representatives' Oversight Committee has voted 24 to 19 to summon former Attorney General Pam Bondi to testify regarding the handling of documents related to convicted financier Jeff...

item.title: Brandon Aura Shapes a New Path in African Folk-Pop Music
item.description: Brandon Aura, a 25-year-old Zimbabwean-South African singer-songwriter, is making waves in African folk-pop by blending heritage, storytelling, and emotional honesty. Influenced by Zimbabwean legend O...

item.title: Bahati Mtomaye: Promoting Fairness and Women Empowerment in Public Service
item.description: Bahati Mtomaye, who is the Assistant Commissioner and Human Resources Manager at the Tanzania Forest Services Agency, has built her career on honesty, dedication, and respect of public service rules. ...

item.title: DILG alleges bribery by BFP chief over ambulance procurement
item.description: The Department of the Interior and Local Government (DILG) has formally accused the chief of the Bureau of Fire Protection (BFP) of being involved in a bribery scheme linked to an ambulance procuremen...

item.title: NASA Restructures Artemis Program Following Artemis II Launch Delays
item.description: In February 2026, NASA’s Artemis II mission faced multiple delays due to technical issues with the Space Launch System (SLS) rocket. A hydrogen leak was detected during the first wet dress rehearsal, ...

item.title: NCC Proposes 14-Day Notice Before SIM Recycle to Protect Users
item.description: The Nigerian Communications Commission (NCC) don set plan to stop wahala wey dey happen when phone numbers dey recycled. Right now, if your SIM no dey active for six months, e fit enter inactive categ...

item.title: Chirag Paswan Criticises Bihar Government Amid Public Rally
item.description: On 26th July, Union Minister Chirag Paswan addressed a public gathering in Gaya, Bihar, where he openly criticised the Nitish Kumar-led NDA government. Speaking in the wake of the ambulance rape incid...

item.title: Vatican criticizes US-Israeli military actions in Iran as a threat to international law
item.description: Vatican Secretary of State Cardinal Pietro Parolin has strongly criticized the ongoing US-Israeli military strikes on Iran, calling them 'truly alarming' for their impact on international law. In an u...

item.title: Iran’s Low-Cost Drones Pose Financial Challenge to US Defence Systems
item.description: The United States is currently dominating the skies over Iran, but the cost dynamics of modern drone warfare are proving challenging. Iran has deployed over 2,000 low-cost Shahed drones since the US a...

item.title: EAC to review Partner States’ contributions based on economic capacity
item.description: The East African Community (EAC) Council of Ministers has been directed to review the current contribution formula used by Partner States. The present model requires each country to contribute $7 mill...

item.title: March Fuel Price Update: Petrol, Diesel, and Kerosene Increase Across Coastal Ports
item.description: Starting March 4th, 2026, fuel prices in Tanzania have increased across all major coastal ports. In Dar es Salaam, petrol now costs Sh2,864 per litre, up Sh76 from February. Diesel experienced a sharp...
